avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations are numerous. As a consequence of new snow and wind, deep wind slabs formed. Natural avalanches are further possible.
Backcountry touring calls for extensive experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and great restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack in all aspects.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The wind will be moderate. Continuous warming to 2800 m.

bulletin.highlights: Even single freeriders can release avalanches very easily.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Outside marked and open pistes a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs remain prone to triggering on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line, caution is to be exercised, including in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers of the snowpack.
Additionally avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size on west, north and east facing slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist loose snow avalanches are possible. This applies especially on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes strong wind will transport the snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack. This applies especially on very steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular in extremely steep terrain. Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and older wind slabs can still be released. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized but can be released in many cases even by a single winter sport participant.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is greater.

The weather will be mild. In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: On very steep sunny slopes and at low altitude medium-sized and, in many cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Here a lot of snow is lying for the time of year. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Moist and wet avalanches are still possible during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. They can in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. The avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size. As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of avalanches being released will increase.
With the freezing level at 2600 metres, combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, some wet and moist avalanches are still possible at medium mountain altitudes on slopes that have not yet been completely cleared in recent days.
Careful route selection is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow fell in the last seven days.
Until Monday the wind will be moderate to strong. Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Some medium-sized slab avalanches have been released by people in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be very warm. The weather conditions will foster a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. The danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs represent the main danger. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of a strong wind from northwesterly directions, large wind slabs formed. They must be evaluated with care and prudence. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. In particular here the avalanches can be released in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
With the freezing level at 2600 metres, combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, some wet and moist avalanches are still possible at medium mountain altitudes on slopes that have not yet been completely cleared in recent days.
Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and caution. Careful route selection is recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
A lot of snow fell in the last seven days, especially along the border with France.
Until Monday the wind will be strong over a wide area.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
In these regions at intermediate and high altitudes a lot of snow is lying for the time of year. Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Some large and, in isolated cases, very large powder avalanches have been released in the last few days. Isolated medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches have been released by people in the last two days.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be very warm. The weather conditions will foster a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. The danger of wet and gliding avalanches will increase.

bulletin.highlights: Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great restraint.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Outside marked and open pistes a very precarious avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow. Above the tree line avalanche prone wind slabs formed, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.
On very steep sunny slopes medium-sized natural avalanches must be expected. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Mostly the dry avalanches are medium-sized and can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 20 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 50 cm in some localities, has fallen since Thursday. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. The meteorological conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.
The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The snowpack remains prone to triggering over a wide area. The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and older wind slabs can still be released. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized but can be released also by a single winter sport participant.

The weather will be mild. In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: On very steep sunny slopes and at low altitude medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days, especially on near-ridge east, south and southwest facing slopes, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In these regions above approximately 1300 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
Shady slopes: The snowpack is dry and has a loosely bonded surface.

The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.

bulletin.highlights: The avalanche danger is within the uppermost range of danger level 3 (considerable).

avalancheActivity.highlights: Shady slopes: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Very steep sunny slopes: Increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above the tree line. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.

The wind has transported some snow. In particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls as well as at elevated altitudes sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m. The runout zones are to be treated with caution.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last few days. The wind has transported some snow.
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to gradual and thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.

tendency.highlights: The activity of wet avalanches will gradually increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Shady slopes: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Very steep sunny slopes: Increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above the tree line. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m. The runout zones are to be treated with caution.

snowpackStructure.comment: 20 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 60 cm in some localities, fell in the last few days. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to gradual and thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.

tendency.highlights: The activity of wet avalanches will gradually increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow require caution. Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and older wind slabs can still be released. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized but can be released also by a single winter sport participant.

The weather will be mild. During the day: On very steep sunny slopes and at low altitude medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. In particular below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days, especially on near-ridge northeast, south and southwest facing slopes, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.
During the day: The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2500 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist avalanches. Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind will be moderate to strong at times in some localities. Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls large surface-area wind slabs formed. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. The avalanche prone locations are easy to recognise.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign. Experience and restraint are required. As the day progresses as a consequence of solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of moist avalanches.

snowpackStructure.comment: In particular above approximately 1900 m clearly visible wind slabs formed. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2300 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1700 m. New snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. A high (level 4) danger of natural avalanches exists.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered. Large to very large natural avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger. Slab avalanches are possible in isolated cases as before.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep sunny slopes. In particular on steep sunny slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack. These conditions will cause a gradual weakening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow requires caution. As a consequence of the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on sunny slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: Soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in areas close to the tree line.
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Sunny slopes: Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.
Since Friday the weather has been very mild. In particular below the tree line: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The wind slabs can be released easily or naturally in particular on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
The fresh snow and in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment: Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m. The runout zones are to be treated with caution.

snowpackStructure.comment: Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to gradual and thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.

tendency.highlights: The activity of wet avalanches will slowly increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

In addition the wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some cases still. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line.

Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported some snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed.

The snowpack will be in some cases prone to triggering. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
The meteorological conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on wind-loaded slopes small and medium-sized slab avalanches are possible. Single backcountry tourers can release avalanches in some places, including medium-sized ones. As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist loose snow avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on steep south facing slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack will consolidate.
